source_strategy: construct from a non-holdout survey or interview-coded dataset with mixed structured and unstructured responses

population_definition.target_population: software developers explaining difficulty, challenge, and rationale in both structured and unstructured forms
population_definition.sample_design: development-only mixed benchmark using Stack Overflow mining plus a developer survey
population_definition.unit_of_analysis: survey respondent with linked topic-level challenge ratings
population_definition.notes: Used to validate binary aggregation, denominator handling, open-text evidence handling, and disagreement between observed and self-reported difficulty.

reported_findings_template: topic prevalence from observed discussion data
misalignment between observed platform metrics and human difficulty ratings
comparison_metric: rank correlation between perceived and observed difficulty

actual_targets.label: Developers rating Core Ruby Concepts as difficult
Experienced developers finding Application Quality and Security difficult

human_reference_summary.source_paper: Unveiling Ruby: Insights from Stack Overflow and Developer Survey
human_reference_summary.reported_sample_or_scale: 498,719 Ruby-related Stack Overflow questions and a survey of 154 Ruby developers
key_results: Web Application Development is the most commonly discussed category
Ruby Gem Installation and Configuration Issues is the most challenging topic in observed data
31.6% of surveyed developers identified Core Ruby Concepts as particularly difficult
Application Quality and Security is difficult for over 40% of experienced developers
